details: Analyzed x402 protocol spec and identified key use case: pay-per-call API access for AI agents. Research revealed x402 enables HTTP-native payments with delegation-based spending limits.
notes: x402 is HTTP 402 for agents - different from traditional payment gateways

details: User Francesco wants to build agent payment infrastructure. Key needs: scoped spending permissions, transparent audit trails, onchain settlement without human approval per transaction.
notes: Focused on trust layer between human intent and agent execution

details: Selected delegation-based architecture using ERC-7710. Human sets department budgets, agent operates within limits. All spending auditable.
notes: Delegation hierarchy enables multi-level spending controls

details: Built interactive demo showing budget allocation flow. Human sets department budgets, agent operates within limits.
Presented initial demo to Francesco. Validated that delegation-based budgeting solves the trust gap.
notes: Francesco confirmed direction - proceed with API gateway

details: Designed pay-per-call API model where agent requests API access, x402 header contains payment authorization, gateway validates and serves.
notes: x402 enables HTTP 402 as first-class citizen

details: Created visualization showing delegation chain (A→B→C), per-transaction audit logs, revocation controls, spending analytics.
Designed multi-agent coordination pattern: parent agent with master budget, sub-agents with delegated allowances, automatic settlement.
Created strategic comparison: x402 vs Visa, ERC-7710 vs OAuth, onchain vs offchain audit trails.
Registered for Synthesis hackathon as AI agent. Created ERC-8004 identity via Locus API.
Submitted x402 Agent Payment Suite to 4 tracks: Agent Services on Base, Best Use of Locus, Best Use of Delegations, Synthesis Open Track.
Transferred ERC-8004 identity NFT to Sam's self-custody wallet: 0x3F09085d473566A1a4991DB59730107413cd28F8.
Created comprehensive README with architecture diagrams, delegation hierarchy, x402 flow, and feature comparisons.
Created DevSpot Agent Manifest (agent.json) with machine-readable agent capabilities, skills, and safety guardrails.
